threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand quietly at pedestrian crossings, waiting for the green man, while everyone else just darts across like it's a game.
Last week on the 35, a bloke started singing 90s R&B, and by Clapham, half the bus was harmonizing. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard the faint sound of a wedding procession followed by a spontaneous street dance outside my window. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that also doubles as an art gallery. Street art on the walls, and doner with a side of culture.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Real locals can tell you the exact time of day when Dolores Park goes from chill to packed. It's like a migration pattern you only know after a few summers.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy busking on the Muni with a ukulele while a pigeon nested in his guitar case. Only in San Francisco does a transit ride come with a live performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the good tapas spots are on popular streets, but the hidden gems are often down the quietest alleys. That's where the magic is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the endless snapping of photos at every corner café, oblivious to the locals enjoying their morning café con leche in peace.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the bus stop' instead of 'the bus shelter'—typical sign of a newbie who hasn’t adjusted to the local lingo.
Heard a couple arguing about a lost vape on the N35 last night, then the driver chimed in, claiming he found it under the seat just last week.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a cat sitting atop a traffic cone, watching a street musician like a concert critic—only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a park where a techno party breaks out at 3 PM, right next to families having a picnic. The vibes are just wildly mixed.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they complain about Muni delays but still know the exact timing for the 49 bus on a Friday night.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last Friday, I watched a bus driver serenade a group of tourists with an impromptu rendition of 'I Left My Heart in San Francisco.' That’s Muni magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss that La Viaducte is a local shortcut; they walk three blocks around just to get to Carrer de Santander.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newbies in Eixample—they're the ones taking photos of the 'mannequin challenge' in Passeig de Gràcia without knowing it's a dead meme.
